_readme: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the model landscape. Consumed by (a) the paper 'Using AI Safely and Ethically in Research', Section 2, and (b) the llm-guide web tool, Model landscape section. Update here only, then run shared/sync-models.sh. This is the fix for the two artefacts drifting apart.
verification_note: All aaIndex figures below were read directly from the Artificial Analysis leaderboard on 2026-07-20, not from secondary reporting. Index version v4.1; scores are NOT comparable across index versions. 150 models ranked, of which 84 are open weights.
important_caveat_scores_are_per_configur: Artificial Analysis scores a model CONFIGURATION, not a model. The same model scores differently at different reasoning-effort settings — e.g. GPT-5.6 Sol scores 59 (max), 58 (xhigh), 56 (high), 54 (medium), 49 (low) and 41 (non-reasoning). Always state the configuration alongside a score. The configuration is given in each entry's name field.
benchmark_policy: Earlier versions of the web tool displayed hardcoded MMLU / HumanEval / MATH figures with no source. These were removed rather than carried forward. A single sourced composite with an explicit as-of date and configuration is more defensible than three unsourced numbers.
caveats: Composite 'intelligence' scores aggregate very different underlying tasks - cite the methodology alongside any score.
Rankings changed materially within a single month during drafting. Every figure here is perishable.
'Open weight' is not 'open source': weights and a licence are published, but often not training data or code. Licences vary per model and must be checked individually.
Openness is a spectrum, not a binary: NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 Ultra appears in open-model comparisons but its weights are listed as not available. Artificial Analysis maintains a separate Openness Index.

models.name: Claude Fable 5 (with fallback)
models.strengths: Ranked #1 of 150 models on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index

models.name: GPT-5.6 Sol (max)
models.strengths: Ranked #2; broad general capability, most widely deployed via ChatGPT

models.name: Kimi K3
models.strengths: Ranked #4 overall - the highest-scoring model from a lab that usually publishes weights
models.note: NOT currently classified as open weights by Artificial Analysis: it does not appear in their open-models comparison, and their top open-weight list is GLM-5.2, MiniMax-M3, DeepSeek V4 Pro. Do not describe K3 as open-weight without checking whether weights have since been released.

models.name: Claude Opus 4.8 (max)
models.strengths: Reasoning, safety, long-context analysis

models.name: GPT-5.5 (xhigh)
models.strengths: Still widely deployed; strong default for writing and knowledge work

models.name: Grok 4.5 (high)
models.strengths: Integrated with the X platform
models.note: Artificial Analysis lists the creator as 'SpaceXAI'.

models.name: Claude Sonnet 5 (max)
models.strengths: Strong cost-performance balance; coding

models.name: GLM-5.2 (max)
models.strengths: Highest-scoring open-weight model on the leaderboard

models.name: Gemini 3.5 Flash
models.strengths: Strong price-performance; scores above Gemini 3.1 Pro on this index

models.name: Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview
models.strengths: Long context, multimodal, deep Workspace integration

models.name: Qwen3.7 Max
models.strengths: Alibaba's top-scoring model; multilingual
models.note: Does not appear in the open-models comparison - treat as proprietary unless confirmed otherwise.

models.name: MiniMax-M3
models.strengths: Joint second-highest open-weight model; very low price

models.name: DeepSeek V4 Pro (Reasoning, Max Effort)
models.strengths: Joint second-highest open-weight model; MoE efficiency, reasoning and code

models.name: Kimi K2.6
models.strengths: Open-weight; agentic coding

models.name: MiMo-V2.5-Pro
models.strengths: Large open-weight MoE at very low price

models.name: DeepSeek V4 Flash (max)
models.strengths: Cheapest capable open-weight option in this set

models.name: Nemotron 3 Ultra 550B A55B (Reasoning)
models.strengths: Appears in open-model comparisons
models.note: Weights listed as 'Not available' despite appearing on the open-models page - a good illustration that openness is a spectrum.

models.name: Qwen3.5 397B A17B (Reasoning)
models.strengths: Alibaba's leading open-weight release; multilingual

models.name: Mistral Medium 3.5
models.strengths: European provenance; dense model, simpler to self-host than MoE

models.name: Claude 4.5 Haiku
models.strengths: Fastest Claude tier; low latency, cost-efficient

models.name: Gemma 4 31B (Reasoning)
models.strengths: Runs on modest hardware - a practical route to keeping sensitive data local

models.name: gpt-oss-120b (high)
models.strengths: OpenAI's open-weight release - a frontier lab publishing weights

models.name: Llama 4 Maverick
models.strengths: Widely supported ecosystem, but now well down the rankings
models.note: Llama 4 Scout supports a 10M context window - the largest on the leaderboard - but scores 10. Meta's highest scorer is now Muse Spark 1.1 (51).

embedding_models._note: Compare on the public MTEB leaderboard rather than vendor claims; match the model to the task (retrieval vs clustering vs classification).

structural_points: A real gap remains between the best proprietary and best open-weight models: 60 (Claude Fable 5) against 51 (GLM-5.2) on the Intelligence Index - about nine points on a 100-point scale, where the top 20 models span roughly 60 to 46.
Open weights are nonetheless the majority of the field: 84 of the 150 ranked models are open weights.
The distinction is no longer 'frontier labs keep weights closed': OpenAI publishes gpt-oss-120b, and Google publishes the Gemma family.
Chinese labs (Z AI/Zhipu, MiniMax, DeepSeek, Moonshot/Kimi, Xiaomi, Alibaba) occupy most of the leading open-weight positions - relevant to procurement, provenance and data-governance decisions.
Open-weight models are markedly cheaper: GLM-5.2 at $0.90 per million tokens against $7.70 for the top proprietary model.
Local open-weight models remain the practical route to using AI on confidential or personal data without it leaving your institution - and a 30B model such as Gemma 4 31B runs on modest hardware.
Context windows have converged: 1M tokens is now common across frontier models.
